_comment: Thin-brief example. User input was a single sentence: 'Make a PPT about butterflies for kids'. No content documents. Most fields are inferred; every inference is logged in `inferences[]` with confidence. `slideCountConstraint` is recorded in s01b (not here); `imageryDemand` is derived in Step 5b (not here).
purposeStatement: Introduce young children to butterflies in a way that sparks curiosity and basic recognition.

audience.who: Young children (ages ~5–8) in a classroom or family setting
audience.priorKnowledge: Novice — may recognize a butterfly visually but unfamiliar with terminology like 'metamorphosis'
audience.concerns: Short attention span; needs strong visuals and simple language

communicationObjectives.think: Butterflies are fascinating insects that grow through clear life-cycle stages
communicationObjectives.feel: Curious and delighted; nature is wonderful
communicationObjectives.do: Point out butterflies and their stages when they see them in the world

keyMessages.message: A butterfly transforms through four life stages: egg → caterpillar → chrysalis → butterfly
Butterflies come in many beautiful colors and live almost everywhere

inferences.basis: topic is educational and audience is children; inform grants more design latitude than report/propose
user said 'kids'; specific age band inferred from typical classroom/family use
default emotional outcome for educational content aimed at children
